constraints.message: bucket and verdict must satisfy the normative pairing table: HIGH_CONSENSUS->proceed; DISPUTED->defer; BLOCKER->reject; LOW_VALUE->{proceed|defer|low_value_pass}.
constraints.evaluation_note: Normative bucket-verdict table pinned in v8.4.0. LOW_VALUE is intentionally permissive (consumer decides pass-through verdict per its threshold policy); other buckets are single-pair.

constraints.message: juror_verdicts must contain between 4 and 16 entries (panel size bounds).
constraints.evaluation_note: Belt-and-braces with the schema's minItems/maxItems; constraint check binds cross-runner conformance.

constraints.message: asymmetric_blocker_signal.validated must equal the disjunction of (cross_model_agreement >= 0.7) OR (same_model_reviewer_score >= 600).
constraints.evaluation_note: Two-condition veto consistency: the resolved boolean and its underlying threshold disjunction must agree.

constraints.message: signing_context must carry non-empty audience and scope, and a well-formed semver contract_version.
constraints.evaluation_note: Pass-3 fix (resolves SDD section 3.6 PV-4). Well-formed semver pattern is enforced at the schema layer via SigningContextSchema.contract_version (^d+\.\d+\.\d+$); the DSL grammar v2.0 has no regex builtin, so this expression bounds the field lengths and the schema pattern carries the format check at validate-time. Cross-version compatibility (MIN_SUPPORTED_VERSION semver-tuple comparison) is consumer-side per NF-1; the library validates only the format.
